reasoning: The Lean formalization encodes all components of the informal statement correctly. Distinctness of the four points A,B,C,D is captured by `S.ncard = 4`. A circle is encoded as `through (r, P) Q := dist P Q = r`, which is precisely the locus of points at distance r from center P in the plane `EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 2)`. The hypothesis `hABCD` quantifies over all pairs (r,P) and (s,Q) such that A,B lie on the first "circle" and C,D lie on the second, asserting existence of a common point—exactly "every circle through A,B intersects every circle through C,D". Degenerate cases (r ≤ 0) are handled vacuously since A ≠ B forces any valid r to be positive. The conclusion correctly splits into collinear (Mathlib's `Collinear ℝ S`) or cocyclic (all four points equidistant from some center P with radius r, which in 2D is precisely lying on a common circle). No extra or missing hypotheses.
